DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS GEORGE CAROLLO, STAYA, INC. D/B/A DOVER'S MOTION TO COMPEL MORE PARTICULARIZED IDENTIFICATION OF TRADE SECRETS PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2019.210, ENFORCE PROTECTIVE ORDER, AND STAY DISCOVERY *** REDACTED VERSION *** IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S TRADE SECRETS DESIGNATION IS NOT REASONABLY PARTICULAR. PLAINTIFF SHALL FURNISH A REVISED DESIGNATION WITHIN 30 DAYS. THE REVISED DESIGNATION SHOULD INCLUDE A SUMMARY IN PLAIN LANGUAGE OF THE SPECIFIC TRADE SECRETS AT ISSUE AND A NUMBERED LIST OF THE TRADE SECRETS WITH A CORRESPONDING LIST OF SPECIFIC ELEMENTS FOR EACH. THE REVISED DESIGNATION NEED NOT EXPLAIN HOW THE ALLEGED TRADE SECRETS DIFFER FROM GENERAL KNOWLEDGE IN THE TRADE BUT MUST INCLUDE ENOUGH DETAIL TO ENABLE DEFENDANTS TO INVESTIGATE AND MAKE THAT DETERMINATION. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO MODIFY THE PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT TO CHALLENGE ANY CONFIDENTIALITY DESIGNATIONS THAT PLAINTIFF MAY MAKE TO THE REVISED DESIGNATION. DISCOVERY BY PLAINTIFF IS STAYED PENDING SERVICE OF THE REVISED DESIGNATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS IN DEPARTMENT 302 FILED BEFORE JUNE 1, 2020 ARE OFF CALENDAR PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S GENERAL ORDERS. TO RE-SCHEDULE THE HEARING, PLEASE REFER TO THE PUBLIC NOTICE TO RESTORE SERVICES ISSUED ON MAY 27, 2020 AVAILABLE ON THE COURT'S WEBSITE AT HTTPS://SFSUPERIORCOURT.ORG/.

Defendants Motion To Compel More Particularized Identification Of Trade Secrets Pursuant To C.C.P. 2019.210, Enforce Protective Order, And Stay Discovery *** Redacted Version ***
Off Calendar Dec-16-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Eugene Mar, esq., Jeffrey Lau, esq., and Ashley Roybal-Reid, esq. (415-954-4415) of Farella Braun + Martel LLP for Plaintiff.
Rebecca Furman, esq. (415-762-7692) and Nathalie Fayad, esq. (415-625-0694) of Lewis & Llewellyn LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs GEORGE CAROLLO, STAYA, INC. D/B/A DOVER's Motion To Compel More Particularized Identification Of Trade Secrets Pursuant To C.C.P. 2019.210, Enforce Protective Order, And Stay Discovery *** Redacted Version *** is Granted. Plaintiff's trade secrets designation is not reasonably particular. Plaintiff shall furnish a revised designation within 30 days. The revised designation should include a summary in plain language of the specific trade secrets at issue and a numbered list of the trade secrets with a corresponding list of specific elements for each. The revised designation need not explain how the alleged trade secrets differ from general knowledge in the trade but must include enough detail to enable Defendants to investigate and make that determination. Defendants' request to modify the protective order is denied, without prejudice to Defendant to challenge any confidentiality designations that Plaintiff may make to the revised designation. Discovery by Plaintiff is stayed pending service of the revised designation.

Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
